diagrams.title: Five Phases of Project Management Life Cycle
diagrams.description: Linear flow diagram showing the progression through 5 phases: Initiation → Planning → Execution → Monitoring & Controlling → Closure. Shows that Monitoring runs parallel to Execution.
elements: Monitoring & Controlling Phase Box (connected to Execution)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the sequential and overlapping nature of project phases

diagrams.title: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Tree-like hierarchical structure showing project broken into major deliverables, then components, then work packages.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how project scope is decomposed into manageable units

diagrams.title: Project Stakeholder Matrix
diagrams.description: 2x2 matrix with Power/Influence on Y-axis and Interest/Support on X-axis, showing four quadrants: Manage Closely, Keep Satisfied, Keep Informed, Monitor.
diagrams.purpose: Understand stakeholder management strategies based on power and interest

diagrams.title: Critical Path Method Network Diagram
diagrams.description: Network diagram showing project activities as nodes with dependencies, highlighting the critical path (longest sequence) with a distinct color.
diagrams.purpose: Identify critical activities that determine minimum project duration

visuals.title: Project Management Life Cycle
visuals.description: Visual diagram for project management life cycle
